research_question: Do corrected thresholds change G-DET operating-point conclusions?
protocol: G-DET-v2 (5 outer stored family folds; per-fold inner GroupKFold(4) OOF threshold on outer-train; refit on full outer-train; freeze; single test eval)
threshold_selection_method: inner family-grouped OOF max-F1 (frozen before test)
metrics: AUPRC AUROC F1 P R FPR + per-row scores persisted
success_criterion: protocol defensible; deltas vs v1 documented
failure_criterion: thresholds fail to transfer (F1 collapse >0.15) -> investigate threshold stability

research_question: Does retained detection under M0-M3 hold with corrected thresholds?
protocol: G-MUT-v2 (same fit; mutate held-out malicious; recall at frozen OOF threshold)
model: usenix rules + blocklist + opcode_xgb + selector_lr + authguard
uncertainty_method: fold std + family bootstrap on per-row flags
success_criterion: ordering of methods unchanged or honestly reported
failure_criterion: authguard M3 recall drops materially below v1 -> report as corrected finding

research_question: Compound M3+flooding breaking point under corrected thresholds
protocol: G-VOL-v2 (M3 recipe + dead-code frac 0/.25/.5/1/2)
transformation_condition: M3+F{0..200} single fixed donor (v1 donor) AND multi-donor arm
metrics: recall vs frac; FPR on flooded negatives (new)
success_criterion: curve reproduced with corrected thresholds

research_question: Implement partition-isolated donor pools with full provenance
protocol: infrastructure (no metric)
dataset: benign donor pools from benign_general (primary) + benign_cleared (check arm) partitioned by family and fold role
success_criterion: per-variant record: recipient source/family/partition + donor source/family/partition + byte-range hash + seed + condition; assertions on both sides

research_question: Does source-balanced augmentation survive donor isolation?
protocol: G-ADV-v2 (identical to v1 but train/val/test variants drawn from partition-isolated multi-donor pools)
threshold_selection_method: val-fold clean-M0 max-F1 (unchanged) OR inner-OOF variant (report both)
transformation_condition: SEEN={M0 M1 M2 F25 F50 F100} HELD={M3 F200}
metrics: AUPRC AUROC P R F1 FPR retained_vs_M0
success_criterion: F200 recall gain CI excludes 0 under isolation
failure_criterion: gain collapses -> report honestly; v1 gain attributed to donor adaptation

research_question: Does augmentation help under the unresolved compound M3+F200?
protocol: G-ADV-v2 extra test condition M3+F200 (never trained)
success_criterion: resolves the paper's open condition either way

research_question: Family-clustered uncertainty for headline claims
protocol: pooled per-row test scores across folds; family bootstrap (retain all rows of sampled families; 10k reps)
metrics: AUPRC CI; paired dAUPRC(AG - best baseline) CI; family-vs-random gap CI
success_criterion: CIs computed for all 3 targets
failure_criterion: AG-vs-baseline CI includes 0 -> soften superiority claim

research_question: Inventory unsupported manuscript claims
protocol: static audit
metrics: claim list with line refs and replacement wording
success_criterion: every flagged sentence has an approved replacement

research_question: What explains the 23 conflicting exact-bytecode groups?
protocol: evidence collection per group (addresses chains labels deploy metadata init/storage diffs external deps artifact evidence) + 5-way categorization
success_criterion: every group categorized (unresolved allowed)

research_question: Is abstention/escalation appropriate for known-conflict bytecodes?
protocol: frozen conflict-hash list as escalation trigger; measure coverage/recall/FPR effect on retained corpus
success_criterion: rule concentrates conflicts without recall loss
failure_criterion: rule fires too broadly (>5% escalation) -> report and drop

research_question: Enable independent human label adjudication
protocol: stratified sampling (random pos; random weak-neg; high-score FP; low-score FN; conflict examples) + anonymized forms + evidence packets + guidelines + agreement scripts
success_criterion: package complete and reproducible; NO claim of performed adjudication

research_question: Do transformations preserve observed execution on tested inputs?
protocol: fork-based EVM replay (foundry/anvil or py-evm) on 5-10 representative contracts; compare original vs M1/M2/M3/F200 on execution success calls targets storage writes transfers return values traces
metrics: per-contract equivalence table over tested executions
success_criterion: >=5 contracts with identical traces original-vs-mutant on tested inputs
failure_criterion: harness infeasible offline -> deliver minimum-implementation report

research_question: FPR and score profile on 797 benign_general at corrected frozen thresholds
protocol: secondary-control eval; models+thresholds frozen from v2 primary protocol before scoring
split: trained on full primary population (like ind_06 but v2 thresholds)
metrics: FPR; score distribution; median; p95; alerts/1000; top-scoring cases
success_criterion: reported regardless of value

research_question: Case observations on 5 benign_AA delegates
protocol: descriptive scoring only
success_criterion: cases described; no rates claimed

metrics: AUPRC AUROC F1 P R FPR

success_criterion: honest comparison under identical folds
failure_criterion: baseline >= AuthGuard AUPRC -> revise contribution claims

research_question: Are conclusions stable across family thresholds 0.75/0.85/0.90?
protocol: G-DET-v2 rerun grouped by family_id_075 and family_id_090 (frozen columns; folds recomputed per grouping; primary frozen 0.85 untouched)
metrics: AUPRC + gap vs random + family diagnostics (counts singletons largest size-dist class-dist cross-class chaining)
success_criterion: conclusion ordering stable across thresholds

research_question: Sensitivity to recomputing families AFTER task alignment
protocol: rerun 01_freeze_families logic on 3082 retained rows (NEW file; frozen artifacts untouched); compare structure + G-DET
metrics: same + Rand index vs frozen families
success_criterion: labeled sensitivity analysis only; frozen primary preserved

research_question: Do conclusions depend on observation weighting?
protocol: reweighted evaluation of pooled v2 per-row scores: observation-weighted; inverse-family-size; one-vote-per-exact-bytecode; family-macro recall/FPR; family-clustered pooled metrics (NO per-family AUPRC averaging)
success_criterion: direction of AG advantage stable under all weightings
failure_criterion: advantage reverses under any weighting -> disclose

research_question: Trivial comparator: truncate at first STOP
protocol: G-DET-v2 + flooding conditions with features computed on bytes before first STOP
success_criterion: defines the bar Gate A must beat

research_question: Does a conservative terminal-aware dual-view representation improve flooding robustness without clean degradation?
protocol: dual-view features: full-view 773 + restricted-view 773 (conservative terminal-aware region) + trailing-byte volume + post-terminal ratio + view feature-diffs + score disagreement; success criteria PREDEFINED in protocol doc before results
transformation_condition: clean M0; G-MUT M1-M3; G-VOL; pure F200; compound M3+F200; G-ADV variants; benign_general
expected_runtime: ~1-2 days incl. dev; time-boxed 4 working days
success_criterion: meaningful flooding improvement AND clean dAUPRC >= -0.01..-0.02 AND no material benign FPR increase AND consistent across folds AND beats first-STOP heuristic
failure_criterion: any criterion fails -> do not include in paper; write failure report

research_question: Can selective escalation concentrate errors in a small escalated subset?
protocol: escalation signals: conflict-hash history; trailing-byte ratio; dual-view disagreement; low margin; family/feature outlier score; transformation indicators. Threshold rule frozen on train/val only
dataset: task_aligned v1 primary + G-VOL + compound conditions
threshold_selection_method: frozen v2 thresholds + escalation cutoffs from val
metrics: auto-coverage %; escalation %; non-escalated recall/FPR; error concentration; escalations/1000
success_criterion: errors concentrate (e.g. >=2x error density in escalated set at <=10-15% escalation) with EIP-7702-specific justification
failure_criterion: concentration weak -> retain framework-only contribution; report failure

research_question: Can the Gigahorse/Datalog reference analyzer run locally within budget?
protocol: setup spike: clone gigahorse-toolchain; Soufflé via Docker; wire USENIX client files (analyze.dl run_analysis.sh main.py env.yaml); smoke-test on 5 bytecodes
success_criterion: decision Option A vs B recorded with evidence
failure_criterion: blocked -> Option B report (blockers; attempted steps; dependency failures; remaining-work estimate)

research_question: Bounded agreement/coverage/timing subset vs AuthGuard
protocol: run reference analyzer on stratified subset (~50-100 contracts incl. M0 originals; optionally mutants)
metrics: coverage %; failures; agreement/disagreement categories; preprocessing+analysis time; hardware; input requirements beyond bytecode
success_criterion: complementarity characterized; NO universal speedup claim
failure_criterion: analyzer coverage too low (<50%) -> report as finding

research_question: Operational workload characterization
protocol: PR curves; recall-FPR curves; workload at selected thresholds; alerts/1000; hypothetical prevalence scenarios 0.1%/1%/5% (labeled hypothetical; no probability claims without calibration)
dataset: pooled v2 per-row scores + benign_general control
success_criterion: every scenario labeled hypothetical

success_criterion: fresh-environment reproduction of headline tables from artifact alone
failure_criterion: licensing blocks corpus -> hashes + reconstruction instructions
